scoring_rule: Hybrid mechanical + LLM. Mechanical rule checks (scripts/rules.py) answer probes where markdown syntax is unambiguous; LLM N=3 unanimous-agreement answers the rest, including mechanical-returned-undetermined cases. See docs/failure-mode-catalog.md v0.3 + v0.3.1 amendments for the taxonomy.

probe_results.question: Does the document mention the word 'nimbus'?
probe_results.ground_truth_basis: Source body Section 1 contains 'nimbus' (italic) and the Glossary Section 4 also lists 'nimbus' as one of nine tokens. Verified visually.

probe_results.question: Is the word 'nimbus' presented as italic or emphasized in the document, whether via rendered italic glyphs, markdown italic syntax (e.g., *nimbus* or _nimbus_), HTML em tags, or any other clear signal of italic/emphasis?
probe_results.ground_truth_basis: Source: Section 1 body uses \textit{nimbus}. Rendered PDF shows nimbus in italic glyphs. Probe accepts any preserved emphasis encoding.

probe_results.question: Does the document mention the word 'stratus'?
probe_results.ground_truth_basis: Source Section 1 (bold) and Section 4 Glossary. Verified visually.

probe_results.question: Is the word 'stratus' presented as bold or strongly emphasized in the document, whether via rendered bold glyphs, markdown bold syntax (e.g., **stratus** or __stratus__), HTML strong tags, or any other clear signal of bold/strong emphasis?
probe_results.ground_truth_basis: Source: Section 1 body uses \textbf{stratus}. Rendered PDF shows stratus in bold glyphs.

probe_results.question: Does the document mention the word 'cumulus'?
probe_results.ground_truth_basis: Source Section 1 (monospace) and Section 4 Glossary. Verified visually.

probe_results.question: Is the word 'cumulus' presented as monospace, code-formatted, or typewriter-style text in the document, whether via rendered monospace glyphs, markdown inline-code syntax (e.g., `cumulus`), HTML code tags, or any other clear signal of monospace/code formatting?
probe_results.ground_truth_basis: Source: Section 1 body uses \texttt{cumulus}. Rendered PDF shows cumulus in monospace font.

probe_results.ground_truth_basis: Source: Section 2 table cell uses \textit{beta}. Rendered PDF shows beta in italic inside the second column of the table.

probe_results.question: Does the document mention a word 'mammatus'?
probe_results.ground_truth_basis: Source contains nine tokens (nimbus, stratus, cumulus, beta, delta, zeta, altocumulus, cirrus, stratocumulus). 'Mammatus' is a real cloud subtype (thematic distractor) but is not in this document.

probe_results.question: Is the word 'epsilon' presented as italic or emphasized in the document, whether via rendered italic glyphs, markdown italic syntax, HTML em tags, or any other clear signal?
probe_results.ground_truth_basis: Source: 'epsilon' appears in the table cell as PLAIN text (not formatted). It is the unformatted control next to 'zeta' which IS formatted. Tests whether the converter or judge falsely attributes formatting that is not present.
